title: Parent-infant synchrony and the construction of shared timing; physiological precursors, developmental outcomes, and risk conditions.

assets.abstract: Synchrony, a construct used across multiple fields to denote the temporal relationship between events, is applied to the study of parent-infant interactions and suggested as a model for intersubjectivity. Three types of timed relationships between the parent and child's affective behavior are assessed: concurrent, sequential, and organized in an ongoing patterned format, and the development of each is charted across the first year. Viewed as a formative experience for the maturation of the socia

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Social Incarnation — intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing, second-person neuroscience.
